focus: Maintainability review of naming consistency, documentation clarity, historical-record boundaries, and operator follow-on clarity

findingDetails.title: Install-guide skill inventory still advertises retired sk-deep-* skill IDs
findingDetails.evidence: SET-UP - AGENTS.md active Current Installation table lists sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review, while adjacent current setup surfaces list deep-research and deep-review.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update the SET-UP - AGENTS.md skill inventory to use deep-research and deep-review so operators copying setup tables do not propagate retired skill IDs.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact Grep/Read evidence found stale names in one install-guide table while root README and install-guide summary surfaces already use current names; separate from prior resource-map, shell placeholder, and completion-status P1s.

noveltyJustification: One new maintainability P2 in a previously unreviewed dimension; no P0/P1 findings and no duplicate of prior active P1s.
edgeCases: Allowed historical mentions were not treated as active findings when packet docs framed them as factual history.
Stale sk-deep-research/sk-deep-review install-guide table is broader maintainability drift, not a duplicate of 079 rename findings.
Existing P1-001, P1-002, and P1-003 remain active but were not duplicated.
ruledOut: No new P1 for deep-agent-improvement root/install-guide references; sampled current surfaces use the new skill name.
No finding for retained @improve-agent or /deep:start-agent-improvement-loop because the packet explicitly keeps those stable.
No duplicate P1 for checklist/task/implementation-summary completion mismatch; P1-003 already covers that traceability gate.
